mission.title: Install and Configure Dify
mission.description: Dify is a production-ready platform for developing agentic workflows, ideal for teams looking to build and deploy AI applications. It offers a self-hosted solution that allows for customization and control over the deployment environment. Key trade-offs include the need for infrastructure management and potential complexity in setup compared to managed solutions.

steps.title: Add the Dify Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Dify Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Dify using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add dify https://charts.dify.ai

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Dify chart repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Dify CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Dify to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langgenius/dify/main/deploy/crds.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs from the Dify repository.

steps.title: Install Dify using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the Dify application in a specified namespace using Helm.

```bash

helm install dify dify/dify --namespace dify --create-namespace --version 0.12.0

```

> This command installs Dify in the 'dify' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) for Dify to demonstrate its functionality.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.ai/v1
kind: DifyWorkflow
metadata:
  name: example-workflow
spec:
  description: Example workflow for Dify
EOF

```

> This command creates a sample workflow in Dify.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.ai/v1
kind: DifyWorkflow
metadata:
  name: example-workflow
spec:
  description: Example workflow for Dify
EOF

steps.title: Verify Dify installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Dify Custom Resource to ensure it is ready.

```bash

kubectl get difyworkflows -n dify

```

> This command lists the Dify workflows in the 'dify' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Dify
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Dify installation and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all workflows and data associated with Dify.

```bash

helm uninstall dify --namespace dify

kubectl delete namespace dify

```

> This command uninstalls Dify from the specified namespace.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Dify
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your workflows. This step will upgrade Dify to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get difyworkflows -n dify -o yaml > backup-difyworkflows.yaml

helm upgrade dify dify/dify --namespace dify --version 0.12.0

kubectl get difyworkflows -n dify

```

> Backup existing workflows.
> This command upgrades Dify to the specified version.
> Verify that the workflows are still present after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get difyworkflows -n dify -o yaml > backup-difyworkflows.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'difyworkflows.dify.ai not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) may not have been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langgenius/dify/main/deploy/crds.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster may not be reachable or the kubeconfig may be misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig file and ensure your cluster is running with 'kubectl cluster-info'.

troubleshooting.title: Workflow not starting
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Dify workflow may not be configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the workflow configuration with 'kubectl describe difyworkflow example-workflow -n dify'.

troubleshooting.title: Helm install fails with 'chart not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Dify Helm repository may not be added or updated.

**Fix:**
Run 'helm repo add dify https://charts.dify.ai' and 'helm repo update'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new workflow types and improved API performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated old workflow types, which may require migration.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update existing workflows to use the new types as per the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get difyworkflows -n dify' showing your workflows with a status of 'Ready'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.ai/v1
kind: DifyWorkflow
metadata:
  name: example-workflow
spec:
  description: Example workflow for Dify
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
